exercises.title: Hello World
exercises.solutionCode: public class Main { public static void main(String[] args) { System.out.println("Hello, World!"); } }

exercises.title: Variables & Data Types
exercises.description: Declare an int a = 10, double b = 3.14, and String name = "Java". Print all three on separate lines.

exercises.title: If-Else Statement
exercises.description: Write an if-else: if int score = 85 is >= 60, print Pass, otherwise print Fail.

exercises.title: For Loop
exercises.description: Print numbers from 1 to 5 using a for loop.
exercises.hint: Use for (int i = 1; i <= 5; i++) { System.out.println(i); }
exercises.solutionCode: public class Main { public static void main(String[] args) { for(int i=1; i<=5; i++) { System.out.println(i); } } }

exercises.title: Array Sum
exercises.description: Create an array int[] nums = {2, 4, 6, 8, 10}. Calculate and print the sum.
exercises.hint: Use a for loop to iterate and add each element to a sum variable.

exercises.title: Method — Max of Two
exercises.description: Create a static method max(int a, int b) that returns the larger number. Call it with max(10, 25) and print the result.
exercises.hint: Use return (a > b) ? a : b; or an if-else inside the method.

exercises.title: String Methods
exercises.description: Given String str = "Hello, Java!", print: its length, the uppercase version, and the substring from index 7.

exercises.title: ArrayList
exercises.description: Create an ArrayList<String>, add "Apple", "Banana", "Cherry". Remove "Banana" and print the remaining list.
exercises.hint: Use .add() to add, .remove("Banana") to remove, then println the list.

exercises.title: Class & Constructor
exercises.description: Create a class Car with fields brand and year, a constructor, and a display() method. Create an instance new Car("Toyota", 2024) and call display() printing Toyota (2024).
exercises.hint: Define Car as a static inner class with a constructor and display() method.

exercises.title: Exception Handling
exercises.description: Write a try-catch block that attempts to parse "abc" as an integer using Integer.parseInt(). Catch NumberFormatException and print Invalid number format!. Then parse "42" successfully and print the result.
